channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Frey issues another veto, rejecting council decision to deny raises for high paid city staff
item.description: Mayor Jacob Frey issued his third veto of the month Thursday, objecting to the Minneapolis City Council’s decision to withhold raises from city employees as a means of reducing the property tax levy increase.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Veteran Daniel Penny, acquitted in NYC subway chokehold, will join Trump's suite at football game
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Daniel Penny, a military veteran who choked an agitated New York subway rider and was acquitted of homicide this week, has been invited by Vice President-elect JD Vance to join Donald Trump's suite at the Army-Navy football game on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US updates a science and technology pact with China to reflect growing rivalry and security threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. has updated a decades-old science and technology agreement with China to reflect their growing rivalry for technological dominance.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi injured, hospitalized while traveling to Luxembourg
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi suffered an injury and has been admitted to a hospital in Luxembourg.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watchdog finds FBI intelligence missteps before Jan. 6 riot, but no undercover agents were present
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI should have done more to gather intelligence before the Capitol riot, according to a watchdog report Thursday that also said no undercover FBI employees were on the scene on Jan. 6, 2021, and none of the bureau’s informants was authorized to participate.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden administration makes final diplomatic push for stability across a turbulent Mideast
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — Top U.S. officials were in the Middle East on Thursday, pushing for stability in Syria and an end to Israel’s 14-month war in the Gaza Strip in a last-ditch diplomatic push by the outgoing Biden administration before President-elect Donald Trump takes office in a few weeks.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate begins final push to expand Social Security benefits for millions of people
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate is pushing toward a vote on legislation that would provide full Social Security benefits to millions of people, setting up potential passage in the final days of the lame-duck Congress. Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer, D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton says he’s open to talking to Biden about preemptive pardon for Hillary Clinton
item.description: “I think if President Biden wanted to talk to me about that, I will talk to him about it, but I don’t think I should be giving public advice on the pardon power,” Bill Clinton said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: READ: Here is Biden's full list of clemencies
item.description: President Biden early Thursday announced that he is pardoning 39 people and commuting the sentences of nearly 1,500 others, setting a new daily record for clemency with a focus on those who were under home confinement during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Balluck, Fox 5

item.title: Trump is named Time's Person of the Year, will ring the New York Stock Exchange bell
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is back in his hometown of New York where he's been honored as Time magazine’s Person of the Year and will ring the opening bell at the New York Stock Exchange.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Ontario to restrict electricity exports to U.S. and bar American-made alcohol if Trump tariffs applied
item.description: Ontario may considering barring American-made alcohol in addition to restricting electricity exports to Minnesota, Michigan and New York state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Two lawmakers race to expand gun laws
item.description: Concerns about gun violence in St. Pete come as State Representative Dr. Joel Rudman and State Senator Randy Fine juggle gun laws in Tallahassee amid running for Congressional districts 1 and 6.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NJ bill looks to make doxxing a crime
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - When someone is doxxed, it can be more than just a leak of their information; it can rob someone of their well-being, physically and mentally.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Biden is rushing aid to Ukraine. Both sides are digging in. And everyone is bracing for Trump
item.description: The grinding war pitting Ukraine against its Russian invaders has escalated ahead of Donald Trump’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects challenge to Boston's school admissions policy
item.description: The court's action was the second time the justices declined to intervene in an admissions program based on geography since their 2023 ruling invalidating affirmative action in higher education.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Social Security beneficiaries at risk of delays amid GOP funding fight
item.description: Social Security beneficiaries could face longer wait times for service next year unless Congress agrees to the White House budget office’s request to increase funding for the Social Security Administration (SSA), the administration that runs the program is warning.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Lara Trump stepping down from RNC amid Senate speculation
item.description: Lara Trump is stepping down as co-chair of the Republican National Committee (RNC) as she considers replacing Florida Sen. Marco Rubio (R), whom her father-in-law, President-elect Donald Trump, picked to be secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5
